question: Could the Large Hadron Collider create a black hole that destroys the Earth?
shape: confident/settled answer resting on a large body of interacting physics knowledge

sources.title: CERN — Will CERN generate a black hole?
sources.author: CERN
sources.role: the institutional answer and its stated grounds

sources.title: LHC Safety Assessment Group report
sources.author: LSAG
sources.role: the formal safety argument with its dependencies

challenge: Probe a confident argument for its dependencies and key considerations; surface the weakest or most speculative links (e.g. cosmic-ray analogy, Hawking radiation assumptions) in an accessible way.
note: Add the cosmic-ray safety-argument sources and any critical commentary to test whether the structure surfaces the real load-bearing assumptions.
